title: Power Function with Fast Exponentiation
description:
Implement pow(x, n), which calculates x raised to the power n. Support negative n by returning 1/(x^|n|). Handle edge cases like x=0 and n=0 correctly.

    

Constraints:
    - -10^4 ≤ x ≤ 10^4
- -2^31 ≤ n ≤ 2^31-1
explanation:
Intuition
Exponentiation by squaring halves the exponent each step. If the current exponent is odd, multiply the result by the base and decrement the exponent. Square the base each iteration and shift n right by one bit. Negative exponents invert the base at the start.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Handle n<0 by inverting x and negating n
2. Main Algorithm: Iterate while n>0: if n is odd multiply result by x; square x; n//=2
3. Return Result: Return result with float math

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(log |n|)
- Space Complexity: O(1) iterative variables

Key Insights
- Bit operations or parity checks detect odd exponents
- Invert once for negative powers to avoid repeating division

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting n can be negative
- Multiplying result after squaring base is order-sensitive
hints: Use bit checks for odd exponents
Invert base when n is negative
